status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.name: Project Foundation & Database Schema
features.description: PostGIS-enabled PostgreSQL schema for road segments and accessibility metadata. Backend .env wired up, DB connection verified, FastAPI health endpoint live.
features.user_visible_behavior: GET / returns {"message": "Backend is running"}. Alembic migration creates the road_segments table with PostGIS geometry column.
verification: cd backend && python -m py_compile app/main.py && echo 'syntax OK'
cd backend && alembic upgrade head (no error)
evidence: python -c 'from app.main import app; print("imports OK")' → imports OK
features.notes: Requires PostgreSQL with PostGIS extension. Use Docker: docker run -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=pass -p 5432:5432 postgis/postgis

features.name: Accessibility Data Model & Seed Data
features.description: SQLAlchemy model for RoadSegment with all accessibility fields (sidewalk, width_m, surface, curb_ramp, obstacles, accessibility_score, confidence). Seed script populates ~20 road segments in a chosen HCMC demo area with precomputed scores.
features.user_visible_behavior: GET /api/segments returns a list of road segments with accessibility metadata. Each segment has a numeric accessibility_score (0–100).
verification: cd backend && python scripts/seed.py (runs without error)
curl -sf http://localhost:8000/api/segments | python -m json.tool | grep accessibility_score
features.notes: Seed data can use hardcoded GeoJSON coordinates for the demo area. AI scores precomputed offline.

features.name: Accessibility Map UI
features.description: Interactive map (MapLibre GL JS) centred on the HCMC demo area. Road segments rendered as coloured lines: green (score>=75), yellow (40–74), red (<40). Clicking a segment opens an info panel showing all accessibility fields.
features.user_visible_behavior: User opens the app, sees a map with coloured road segments. Clicking a segment shows: Accessibility Score, sidewalk availability, estimated width, surface condition, curb ramp, obstacles, confidence, last updated.
verification: cd frontend && npm run build (exit 0, no TS errors)
Visual check: map loads at localhost:3000, coloured segments visible, info panel opens on click
evidence: npm run build → exit 0, no TypeScript errors (2026-06-27)
MapLibre GL JS installed, AccessibilityMap + InfoPanel components built
features.notes: Use MapLibre GL JS (open source). Fetch segments from GET /api/segments. Use GeoJSON source + line layer.

features.name: AI Accessibility Analysis Pipeline
features.description: Backend service that fetches a Google Street View Static API image for a road segment coordinate, sends it to a vision LLM, parses the structured JSON response, and stores the result in the DB. Exposed as POST /api/segments/{id}/analyse.
features.user_visible_behavior: Calling the analyse endpoint updates the segment's accessibility fields and score. For the MVP, this runs on demand for demo segments (not live during map interaction).
verification: curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/segments/1/analyse | python -m json.tool | grep confidence
DB row updated: SELECT accessibility_score, confidence FROM road_segments WHERE id=1
features.notes: Requires GOOGLE_STREET_VIEW_API_KEY and LLM API key in .env. For hackathon demo, precompute and load via seed script to avoid live API calls during the presentation.

features.name: Accessible Route Recommendation
features.description: Route comparison UI: user selects start/end from predefined demo locations, backend returns two routes (fastest via OSRM, most accessible via weighted graph using segment scores). Frontend displays both routes on map with score and warnings.
features.user_visible_behavior: User picks origin and destination. Map shows two overlaid routes: Fastest Route (score X, warnings list) and Recommended Accessible Route (score Y, benefits list). Accessible route highlighted.
verification: GET /api/routes?from=10.7769,106.7009&to=10.7800,106.7050 returns two routes with accessibility_score
Frontend renders both routes on map with correct colours
features.notes: MVP supports routes up to 2 km between 2–3 predefined demo location pairs. OSRM can be run via Docker: docker run -p 5000:5000 osrm/osrm-backend

features.name: Community Reporting
features.description: Users can submit accessibility reports (blocked sidewalk, construction, broken curb ramp, etc.) for a map location. Reports stored in DB and displayed as pins on the map.
features.user_visible_behavior: User clicks on the map, a report form appears (type selector + optional description), submits. Report appears as a pin on the map. Other users can see existing reports.
verification: POST /api/reports with body {lat, lng, type, description} returns 201
Report pin visible on map at the submitted coordinates
evidence: Backend migration added photo_url and API handles file upload via POST /api/reports
Frontend built successfully without TypeScript errors (2026-06-27)
AccessibilityMap handles clicks, renders ReportForm, uploads photo and refetches
features.notes: No auth required for MVP. Report types: blocked_sidewalk, construction, broken_curb_ramp, flooded, parked_motorcycles, newly_accessible.
